_metadata.description: NGAI agent template with tier guidance; keep it concise and delete unused optional sections.
_metadata.instructions: Fill only what you need. Keep Tier 1 for simple agents; add Tier 2/3 if warranted.
_metadata.usage: JSON = structured schema; MD = narrative. Delete sections not used.

tier_guidance.tier_1_core: Essential for any agent (agent_type, implementation for chosen type, io_contract, primary_data if data-driven, dependencies, validation)
tier_guidance.tier_2_recommended: For production/shared agents (operational_guidance, error_handling, logging_observability, configuration)
tier_guidance.tier_3_optional: Only if needed (common_patterns, domain_specific_patterns, decision_rules, constraints, tests, cross_references, changelog)

agent_details.description: One-sentence description of what this agent does.

agent_type.description: Select exactly one; delete irrelevant implementation branches.
agent_type.notes: Choosing a type helps decide which optional sections to keep.

rule_based.default_action: What happens if no rules match

primary_data.description: Core structured data (prompts, mappings, rules, endpoints)

validation.description: How to verify the agent works

best_practices.description: Instrument on_tool_call, on_tool_result, and on_agent_end hooks for observability; use hooks for metrics, logging, and guardrail triggers rather than embedding logic in tools
Keep tool count to 10-20 per agent; beyond this threshold model performance degrades. Split large tool sets across specialized sub-agents.

Use low temperature (0.0–0.2) for agents that rely on deterministic tool selection and parameter generation. Higher temperatures increase tool call variability — the model may choose different tools or generate different parameter values for identical inputs. Conversational agents can use higher temperatures (0.7+) for natural variation. Document the temperature choice in implementation.llm_agent.parameters with a note on why.

known_failures.cause: Model repeatedly calls tools without reaching a stopping condition
known_failures.mitigation: Set max_turns limit; use tool_choice=auto (not required) after first tool call; implement loop detection in tool runner

known_failures.cause: Server-side sampling loop has a default limit of 10 iterations; when reached, API returns stop_reason: 'pause_turn' instead of a final answer
known_failures.mitigation: On receiving stop_reason: 'pause_turn', resend the API response as a new user message to allow the agent to continue and complete its task

known_failures.cause: Very large or deeply nested JSON schemas provided to structured output or tool parameter definitions are silently rejected or cause API errors
known_failures.mitigation: Flatten nested schemas where possible; split complex types into multiple simpler tools or output types; validate schema against platform limits before deploying

fallbacks.description: When token usage approaches the context window limit during a long-running agentic task, enable automatic compaction (context summarization) to allow the task to continue without losing progress. Alternative: pause and resume via session ID if the platform supports it.

patterns.name: tool_use_behavior
patterns.description: Control whether the agent continues after a tool call or uses the tool result as the final response.

before.description: Default: LLM processes tool results and generates a follow-up response (agentic loop continues)

after.description: First tool output becomes the final response — useful when the tool result IS the answer (e.g., database lookup)

patterns.notes: Four approaches: 'run_llm_again' (default agentic loop), 'stop_on_first_tool' (tool result = final response), StopAtToolName (stop when specific tool called), custom function (inspect tool results and decide dynamically).

guardrails.description: Run input validation before agent processes user message; run output validation before returning result to caller
guardrails.implementation: parallel guardrail checks (OpenAI pattern) or pre/post hooks (Anthropic pattern)

changes: Added stop_sequences to implementation.llm_agent.parameters (Anthropic + Google convergence)
Added low_temperature_for_tool_agents to operational_guidance.best_practices (3-platform convergence: Anthropic, Google, xAI)
Added server_tool_loop_limit and schema_too_complex_for_structured_output to error_handling.known_failures
Added context_window_exhaustion fallback to error_handling.fallbacks (first entry)
Added tool_use_behavior pattern to common_patterns.patterns (first entry)
Added tool_choice, response_format, disable_parallel_tool_use to implementation.llm_agent.parameters (4-platform convergence)
Added strict field to llm_agent.tools tool definition schema
Added infinite_tool_loop known failure entry to error_handling.known_failures
Added lifecycle_hooks and tool_count_limit entries to operational_guidance.best_practices
